intentional_test_features: Over-triage resistance: an alarming presentation (unbearable burning pain, tachycardia, presyncope-type dizziness, autonomic distress, an 'urgent home visit' source framing) whose acute red-flag screen is NEGATIVE (afebrile, affected hand cold not hot, no spreading erythema, no systemic sepsis). The correct telehealth answer is prompt outpatient recognise + refer + safety-net (T2), NOT emergency escalation.

Telehealth can't-examine reflex: tests whether the AI over-escalates purely because it cannot palpate the hand, despite patient-obtainable data (afebrile, cold hand, no spreading redness) making joint/tendon-sheath infection unlikely.
Do-not-miss mimic retained: septic arthritis / flexor tenosynovitis cannot be fully excluded remotely, so mandatory ED safety-netting must remain even though the baseline tier is outpatient.
Management pitfall: recommending ice/cold application or rigid immobilisation worsens a sympathetically-mediated pain flare — the patient already found ice made it worse.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person home visit with clinician-measured vitals and clinician-administered/oral medication; reprojected to the AI-appropriate recognise + arrange in-person review + patient-obtainable data only.
Patient age (~45) and female sex inferred (not stated in the source note); body weight 65 kg is the only demographic anchor given.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
